# `PRT-Benchmark-Anon`

Public benchmark repository snapshot generated mechanically from the private benchmark workspace.

This folder has 9 direct subfolders and 10 direct files including this README.

## Direct subfolders

- `1.test-prompts/` — Prompt templates used to run the public benchmark tasks.
- `2.test-files/` — Benchmark fixtures referenced by the prompt templates.
- `3.test-sessions/` — Container folder for the PRT-Benchmark-Anon/3.test-sessions subdirectories.
- `4.extracted-data/` — Working folder for PRT-Benchmark-Anon/4.extracted-data.
- `5.analytics/` — Working folder for PRT-Benchmark-Anon/5.analytics.
- `6.TTT2-Artifacts/` — Working folder for PRT-Benchmark-Anon/6.TTT2-Artifacts.
- `7.scoring/` — Deterministic verdict-derivation layer and answer-key artifacts used to score the released CSVs.
- `docs/` — Working folder for PRT-Benchmark-Anon/docs.
- `lean/` — Lean-focused folder for PRT-Benchmark-Anon/lean.

- `README.md` — Autogenerated directory guide for PRT-Benchmark-Anon with subfolder and file-level notes.

## Preserved prior README notes

<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README START -->
# PRT-Benchmark

This repository snapshot is generated mechanically from the benchmark workspace.

Included material:
- prompt and test-file assets;
- curated benchmark-local Lean answer-evidence assets;
- curated TTT2 / CeTA artifact material for Schema A, SANS, Schema B, and Test 01;
- flattened public test-session folders under `5.test-sessions`;
- published extracted-data outputs under `6.extracted-data/csv`, with schemas under `6.extracted-data/schemas` and normalization provenance under `6.extracted-data/normalization`; 
- the generated scoring layer under `7.scoring`, including `7.scoring/answer-key/answer_key.json`; 
- generated paper-number regeneration scripts and markdown outputs under `8.analytics`; 
- a root-level copy of `models-list.md`;
- a root-level `croissant.json` (Croissant 1.0 + RAI metadata) regenerated mechanically on every build by `build_8_croissant.py`;
- root-level `LICENSE`, `DATASHEET.md`, `DATA_STATEMENT.md`, `CITATION.cff`, and `CHANGELOG.md` produced by template substitution from `3.public-repo/side_artifact_templates/` (hand-edits to these files are preserved on rebuild);
- a `docs/index.html` GitHub Pages landing page with embedded JSON-LD dataset metadata.

## Top-level folders

| Folder | Mode | Source |
|---|---|---|
| 1.test-prompts | full-copy | test-prompts |
| 2.test-files | full-copy | test-files |
| 3.lean | curated-public-lean-copy | lean |
| 4.TTT2-Artifacts | curated-answer-ttt2-copy | Artifacts |
| 5.test-sessions | flattened-test-session-bundle | schema-test-A-new-system-tests; schema-test-A-tests; schema-test-B-tests; test-01-kernel-tests; test-02-completion-tests-nat-lex; test-03-completion-tests-ordinal; test-04-measure-verification-tests; test-05-candidate-class-reasoning-tests; test-06-branch-realism-tests |
| 7.scoring | public-scoring-scripts | data_master_consolidation/scripts/answer_verdict_derivation |
| 6.extracted-data | extracted-data-bundle | data_master_consolidation |
| 8.analytics | paper-number-regeneration-scripts | data_master_consolidation/raw_consolidations_data/paper_scripts |

## Notes

- `3.lean` copies only the benchmark Lean modules that directly define test fixtures, prove answer-key rows, or exhibit counterexamples used by scoring; broader external theory modules are deliberately excluded.
- `5.test-sessions` keeps each test folder mechanical and public-safe by excluding support and admin-only directories such as `1.analytics/`, `2.plots/`, and `3.extraction/`, and flattening source `4.test-sessions/` into the destination test root.
- `6.extracted-data/csv/*.csv` copies the nine live benchmark `final_*.csv` exports and renames each file to its public test-folder slug.
- `6.extracted-data/schemas/*schema.md` copies the live `MASTER_SCHEMAS/` files with `MASTER_SCHEMA` renamed to `schema` in the destination filenames.
- `7.scoring/` is generated from `data_master_consolidation/scripts/answer_verdict_derivation/`; `7.scoring/answer-key/` copies the live public answer-key files directly from `data_master_consolidation/raw_consolidations_data/`.
- `6.extracted-data/normalization/` is a fresh copy of the full live normalization folder from `data_master_consolidation/normalization/`, split into `guides/`, `method-maps/`, and `overrides/`; removed source files disappear from the destination on rebuild.
- `8.analytics/` is generated from `data_master_consolidation/raw_consolidations_data/paper_scripts/` and then executed to regenerate the public paper-number markdown outputs.
- `croissant.json` describes every released file or file-collection (9 CSV-backed RecordSets, 2 JSON-backed transcript RecordSets, answer-key FileObjects, plus FileSets for non-tabular folders), regenerated from live data sources on every rebuild. SHA256 + contentSize values always match the released artifacts. Switch the hosting target between GitHub raw URLs and Harvard Dataverse DOI URLs by editing `[hosting]` in `3.public-repo/croissant_config.toml`.
- `LICENSE`, `DATASHEET.md`, `DATA_STATEMENT.md`, `CITATION.cff`, and `CHANGELOG.md` are produced by template substitution from `3.public-repo/side_artifact_templates/`. The builder leaves hand-edited destination files untouched.
- `docs/index.html` is the GitHub Pages landing page with embedded `application/ld+json` metadata for crawler discoverability.
- `public_repo_manifest.json` preserves provenance for the generated snapshot.
<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README END -->
